title: Pan-Seared American Gruy9 Roast
description: A silky, mustardy solo-dinner featuring a tender gruy9 roast, perfect for the American palate. Serve immediately, garnished with fresh herbs.
instructions: Season the gruy9 roast with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and a pinch of mustard seeds, and place it on the roasting pan with rack.
Heat the olive oil in the ceramic tagine over medium-high heat, then sear the gruy9 roast on all sides until browned, about 5 minutes total.
Transfer the tagine to the preheated oven and roast the gruy9 for 20 minutes per pound, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 1355F (575C) for medium-rare.
While the roast is cooking, mix the Dijon mustard, thyme, and rosemary in a small bowl to make the crust.
After the roast has cooked for 20-25 minutes, brush the crust mixture evenly over the top of the gruy9, and continue cooking for another 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let the gruy9 rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing it thinly and serving it hot.
To serve, garnish with fresh thyme and rosemary, and spoon the gravy browning sauce over the top of the gruy9.
For an extra-glazed crust, increase the oven temperature to broil (high) and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, watching closely to avoid burning the crust.
Discard the pan drippings and serve with your choice of sides, such as roasted vegetables or creamy mashed potatoes.
Note: If using a meat thermometer, insert it into the thickest part of the gruy9 roast, avoiding any fat or bone.
As always, let the meat rest for a few minutes before slicing to allow the juices to redistribute and the meat to retain its tenderness.
